noResultsSingle.title: No widget has been configured
noResultsSingle.description: Click here to configure a widget

type.description: Choose the widget type you want to configure.

fields.title: Fields
fields.description: Text data with content and a label

person-card.title: Person card
person-card.description: A person with their key details and contact info

highlight.title: Highlight
highlight.description: A small widget that highlights a single value or a count

generic.title: Generic widgets
generic.description: Broadly applicable — wire them up to any data structure (fields, tables, collections). More configuration, but no limits on what they can show.

specific.title: Specific widgets
specific.description: Each is built for one kind of data — a person, a count, a single value. Fewer options to configure, but only suitable for that purpose.

width.description: Select the desired width for the widget.

medium.title: Medium
medium.subtitle: Contains up to 2 columns of content

large.title: Large
large.subtitle: Contains up to 3 columns of content

xtraLarge.title: Xtra large
xtraLarge.subtitle: Contains up to 4 columns of content

appearance.description: Select the appearance for the widget.

density.description: Select the desired density for the widget

content.description: Enter the content for each column. Keep in mind that not all fields are the same height. For the best result make sure items are properly distributed.
content.widgetTitleTooltip: Title that displays at the top of the widget

fields.currencyCodeTooltip: The currency code such as 'EUR', 'USD' etc. The default value is 'EUR'
fields.digitsInfoTooltip: Represents how many decimals should be displayed via this format: {minIntegerDigits}.{minFractionDigits}-{maxFractionDigits}. minIntegerDigits: Minimum number of digits before the decimal point; Default is 1. minFractionDigits: Minimum number of digits after the decimal point; Default is {{minFractionDigits}}. maxFractionDigits: Maximum number of digits after the decimal point; Default is {{maxFractionDigits}}.
fields.displayTooltip: The format for the currency indicator. One of the following: 'code' for showing the code (i.e. EUR); 'symbol' for showing the symbol (i.e. €); 'string' for just displaying the code as a string. The default option is 'symbol'
fields.dateFormatTooltip: A date format supported by Moment.js (https://momentjs.com/docs/). Default is 'DD-MM-YYYY'
fields.dateTimeFormatTooltip: A date format supported by Moment.js (https://momentjs.com/docs/). Default is 'DD-MM-YYYY, h:m:s'
fields.enumValueTooltip: The value of the property you're looking to change.
fields.enumDisplayTooltip: How the value should be displayed
fields.title: Title
fields.valueTooltip: A path which leads to the property you want to show. For the document's JSON schema, follow this example: 'doc:customer.firstName'. For document properties, follow these examples: 'case:createdBy', or 'case:sequence', or 'case:assigneeFullName'. Note: When there are special characters in a property, the property must be enclosed in quotes. For example: case:"loan-accepted".
fields.ellipsisCharacterLimitTooltip: Enter the number of characters that will display before an ellipsis is shown
fields.linkTextTooltip: The text that will be displayed for the link. If left empty, the URL will be used as link text.
fields.defaultSortWarning: Updating this will overwrite other default sortings.

map.keyTooltip: Value resolver path to GeoJSON data

person-card.description: Choose the person to render and which sections to show on the card.

table.defaultPageSizePlaceholder: Choose amount of rows per page
table.defaultPageSizeTooltip: Number of entries displayed on each page
table.collectionPlaceholder: Specify the path to table data
table.collectionTooltip: A path which leads to the collection of values which you want to display. For showing properties in the document's JSON schema, follow this example: 'doc:children'. Note: When there are special characters in a property, the property must be enclosed in quotes. For example: doc:"loans-accepted".
table.firstColumnAsTitle: First column is the title of the row
table.valueTooltip: Name of the property you wish to access within the previously filled table data path

collection.defaultPageSizePlaceholder: Choose amount of cards per page
collection.defaultPageSizeTooltip: Number of entries displayed on each page
collection.collectionPlaceholder: Specify the path to collection data
collection.collectionTooltip: A path which leads to the collection of values which you want to display. For showing properties in the document's JSON schema, follow this example: 'doc:children'. Note: When there are special characters in a property, the property must be enclosed in quotes. For example: doc:"loans-accepted".
collection.valueTooltip: Name of the property you wish to access within the previously filled collection data path

source.label: Items source
source.tooltip: Path expression resolving to an array. Each element of the array becomes one step in the timeline. Example: iko:/statushistorie

titlePath.label: Title path
titlePath.tooltip: Path applied to each item to extract its title. Example: /statustype/omschrijving

labelPath.label: Tooltip text path (optional)
labelPath.tooltip: Optional path applied to each item to extract the text shown in the step's info tooltip (the icon next to the title). Example: /statustype/toelichting

completedPath.label: Completion timestamp path
completedPath.tooltip: Path applied to each item to extract the timestamp at which the step was reached. Expected as an ISO-8601 timestamp, with or without a timezone offset (e.g. 2025-08-15T10:30:00, 2025-08-15T10:30:00Z, or 2025-08-15T10:30:00+02:00). Items whose value is empty or unparseable are rendered as not yet reached. Example: /datumStatusGezet

highlight.valueHelper: Shows the value at this path. If it resolves to an array, the count of items is shown instead.

filters.description: Define the filters that should appear above the interactive table.
filters.title: Title
filters.keyTooltip: A unique key by which the filter is identified. If the key is not unique, you will not be able to save the filter.
filters.dataTypeTooltip: The data type decides what input is shown to the user. For example, for the data type 'Date', a date picker is shown.
filters.fieldTypeTooltip: The field type decides the values the user can input. For the option 'Single', the user can only input a single value. For the option 'Range', the user may input two values, and these values, and all the values in between, are searched for.
filters.dropdownDataProviderTooltip: The dropdown data source determines where the dropdown values come from.

displayConditions.description: Configure conditions to control when this widget is shown. If you do not add any conditions, the widget is always displayed.

searchFields.noneConfigured: No search fields have been configured for this document type.

searchFieldsTooltips.datetimeRange: A date and time range field

searchFieldsOverview.title: Title
searchFieldsOverview.deleteSearchFieldConfirmation: Are you sure you want to delete this search field?
searchFieldsOverview.noSearchfieldsDescription: There are no search fields configured for this case type.

searchFieldsOverviewTooltips.title: The title is an optional field that appears as a label for the current search field. When the title is not set, the default value will be the search field key translation (if there is no translation available, the original key value will be displayed).
searchFieldsOverviewTooltips.key: A unique key by which the search field is identified. If the key is not unique, you will not be able to save the search field.
searchFieldsOverviewTooltips.path: A path which leads to the property you want to search on. For searching in the document's JSON schema, follow this example: 'doc:customer.firstName'. For searching in the document properties, follow these examples: 'case:createdBy', or 'case:sequence', or 'case:assigneeFullName'. Note: When there are special characters in a property, the property must be enclosed in quotes. For example: case:"loan-accepted".
searchFieldsOverviewTooltips.objectsApiPath: A path which leads to the property you want to search on. For searching in the objects API JSON, follow this example: 'object:/customer/firstName'.
searchFieldsOverviewTooltips.dataType: The data type decides what input is shown to the user. For example, for the data type 'Date', a date picker is shown.
searchFieldsOverviewTooltips.fieldType: The field type decides the values the user can input. For the option 'Single', the user can only input a single value. For the option 'Range', the user may input two values, and these values, and all the values in between, are searched for.
searchFieldsOverviewTooltips.matchType: The match type decides how values in the database are matched to the search values. For the match type 'Exact', the value '1' would only be matched to the value '1'. For the match type 'Contains', the value '1' would be matched to the value '1', '123', and all other values containing '1'.
searchFieldsOverviewTooltips.dropdownDataProvider: The dropdown data source determines where the data for the dropdown comes from.
searchFieldsOverviewTooltips.configFile: Use a config file with dropdown values. The config value must be present on the server
searchFieldsOverviewTooltips.dropdownListValues: Set custom values for the dropdown list. The value shown in the dropdown list should be entered in the right text-field. The left text-field should contain the id of that value.
searchFieldsOverviewTooltips.required: Decides whether the user must enter a value before a search can be executed.

listColumn.title: Title
listColumn.titleTooltip: If this field is filled in, this title will display as the column header, overriding any available translations.
listColumn.keyTooltip: A unique key by which the column is identified. If the key is not unique, you will not be able to save the column.
listColumn.pathTooltip: A path which leads to the property you want to show. For the document's JSON schema, follow this example: 'doc:customer.firstName'. For document properties, follow these examples: 'case:createdBy', or 'case:sequence', or 'case:assigneeFullName'. Note: When there are special characters in a property, the property must be enclosed in quotes. For example: case:"loan-accepted".
listColumn.objectsApiPathTooltip: A path which leads to the property you want to search on. For searching in the objects API JSON, follow this example: 'object:/customer/firstName'.
listColumn.displayTypeTooltip: This option decides how the data is eventually displayed in the table.
listColumn.defaultSortTooltip: If this field has a value, the table will be sorted by default on this property, in the direction specified. Only one column at a time can have this property. On creating a new column, if another column already has a default sort specified, this input field will be disabled.
listColumn.exportTooltip: Only fields from the document or the case can be exported. If another type of field is selected, the export button will be set to ‘disabled’.
listColumn.hasEnvConfigWarningTitle: Case type already has a column configuration
listColumn.hasEnvConfigWarning: This case type already has a column configuration in the environment configuration. Delete this in order to add columns here.
listColumn.noConfigWarning: There are no columns configured for this case type.
listColumn.dateFormatTooltip: A date format supported by Moment.js (https://momentjs.com/docs/).
listColumn.uniqueKeyWarning: The key for this column must be unique.
listColumn.enumTooltip: The keys and values used to transform display data in the table.
listColumn.enumTooltipOptional: The values used to transform display data in the table.
listColumn.deleteColumnConfirmation: Are you sure you want to delete this column?
listColumn.tagAmount: How many tags should be displayed
listColumn.tagAmountTooltip: The amount of tags must be a positive number bigger than zero.

caseMigration.warningBeta: Case migration is still in beta. Migrating cases might give unexpected results. Please use with care.
caseMigration.patchTooltip: The old case can be migrated to the new case by using patches. Patches work by moving case properties from the source location to the target location. For example: source: '/oldLocation/firstName', target: '/newLocation/firstName'. A property can be removed by leaving the target empty. For example: source: '/gulden', target: ''. A property can be given a default value in the source field. For example, source: 'John', target: '/firstName'. A patch will always try to convert the type to the target type. For example, source: '/my/number', target: '/my/text'. A property can be set to [NULL] by using SpEL expressions. For example, source: '${null}', target: '/driversLicense'. A Spring bean can be called for more elaborate patches. For example, source: ${myBean.getAge(source.person.birthYear)}, target: '${myOtherBean.doMore(builder, sourceValue)}'
caseMigration.modalContent: Are you sure you migrate the cases? Some changes can not be undone.
